title: The Dirge.
content:
Old winter was gone
    In his weakness back to the mountains hoar,
    And the spring came down
    From the planet that hovers upon the shore

    Where the sea of sunlight encroaches
    On the limits of wintry night; -
    If the land, and the air, and the sea,
    Rejoice not when spring approaches,
    We did not rejoice in thee,
    Ginevra!

    She is still, she is cold
    On the bridal couch,
    One step to the white deathbed,
    And one to the bier,
    And one to the charnel - and one, oh where?
    The dark arrow fled
    In the noon.

    Ere the sun through heaven once more has rolled,
    The rats in her heart
    Will have made their nest,
    And the worms be alive in her golden hair,
    While the Spirit that guides the sun,
    Sits throned in his flaming chair,
    She shall sleep.
meaning:
"The Dirge." is a quintessential example of Percy Bysshe Shelley's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
